frameworks.name: Theory-Based Reinforcement Learning
frameworks.one_line: Model-based RL using intuitive theories of objects, agents, and interactions

frameworks.name: Bayesian program learning
frameworks.one_line: Probabilistic inference over programs for a game-engine simulator

frameworks.name: Model-based reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: Strong form of model-based RL with internal simulation for planning

frameworks.name: Model-free reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: Standard machine RL requiring vast experience, contrasted with human learning

core_question: How can an artificial agent learn many video games with human-like efficiency and generalization?
key_assumptions: Humans rely on rich abstract causal models of objects and intentional agents
Environments can be represented as probabilistic generative programs over a game-engine simulator
Object-based relational structure supports efficient exploration and planning
manipulations: Compared agent performance across 90 Atari-style video games
Evaluated generalization to new game situations and new levels
key_findings: EMPA closely matches human learning efficiency on 90 Atari-style games
Agent learns new games in minutes of game play
Robust generalization to new game situations and new levels
Captures fine-grained structure in human exploration trajectories and learning dynamics
interpretations: Intuitive-theory-based generative models enable human-level rapid learning and planning
Object-based, relational exploration with internal simulation bridges human and machine RL
